name: Orecchiette Pasta with Sausage, Broccolini, and Roasted Red Peppers
description: A quick weeknight pasta with bold Italian sausage, earthy broccolini, and subtly sweet roasted red peppers tossed in orecchiette with lemon and Parmesan.

steps: In a large high-sided skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the sausage and shallot and cook, breaking up the meat with the back of a spoon, until the meat is cooked through and browned, about 7 minutes.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the broccolini and garlic. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the broccolini is tender, 4 to 5 minutes.
Pour the reserved pasta water into the skillet and use a wooden spoon to scrape up all the brown bits from the bottom of the pan. Season with the salt and pepper.
Add the cooked pasta, roasted red peppers, lemon zest, and lemon juice to the pan and toss to combine. Cook to warm the red peppers, about 2 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning with more salt and pepper if necessary, and serve with a sprinkling of Parmesan cheese, if desired.

notes: Use brown rice pasta for a gluten-free option.

name: Chipotle Chicken Tostadas with Pineapple Salsa
description: Crispy tostadas layered with smoky chipotle chicken, cool avocado, and a bright pineapple salsa. A super easy weeknight meal with tons of flavor.

steps: In a medium bowl, toss together the pineapple, onion, jalapeño, lime juice, garlic, cilantro, avocado oil, and salt. Refrigerate until ready to serve, up to 5 days.
Preheat the oven to 350°F and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

In a large skillet, heat the avocado oil over medium-high heat. Add the ground chicken, chipotle chili powder, salt, and pepper. Cook the chicken, breaking up the meat with the back of a spoon, until it is brown, about 7 minutes. Drain off any excess fat from the pan, if necessary.
Reduce the heat to medium and add the chicken broth and tomato paste and stir to combine. Continue to cook for about 2 more minutes.
Remove from heat and cover to keep warm until ready to serve.
Top tostada shells with chipotle chicken and pineapple salsa.

storage.refrigerate: Pineapple salsa keeps for up to 5 days.

name: One-Pan Mexican Chicken and Rice
description: The most requested recipe in the family — easy, full of flavor, and a one-pan meal everyone adores. Cubed chicken thighs with rice, tomatoes, and green chiles cooked together in one skillet.

steps: In a large skillet with tall sides, heat the oil over medium heat. When hot, add the diced onion and cook, stirring, until tender, about 4 minutes. Increase heat to medium-high and add the cubed chicken, salt, pepper, oregano, garlic powder, paprika, and cumin. Cook, tossing occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through, about 8 minutes.

Add the uncooked rice and cook, stirring, to toast the rice a bit, about 4 minutes. Add the tomato paste, stir until combined, and cook for 1 more minute. Pour in the broth and the can of diced tomatoes and green chiles. Stir to combine and bring to a boil.
Once boiling, reduce heat to low, cover, and let cook until the rice is tender, about 25 minutes.
Serve as is, or topped with sliced avocado, radishes, jalapeño, sliced green onions, and a wedge of lime if desired.

name: Turkey Taco Skillet Bake
description: A super-easy Tex-Mex skillet supper with ground turkey, lots of chopped veggies, a delicious combination of spices, and a little cheese, all baked until warm and bubbly.

steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F.

Heat the olive oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium heat. Add the zucchini, bell pepper, onion, poblano pepper, and garlic. Season with the salt and pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.

Add the ground turkey, paprika, chili powder, oregano, cumin, and cayenne pepper. Cook the turkey, breaking it up with the back of a wooden spoon, until it is cooked through and no longer pink, about 5 minutes.

Reduce the heat to medium and stir in the tomato paste until it is incorporated with the meat mixture. Add half the broth and continue cooking while stirring until the liquid has reduced, about 2 minutes.

Remove the skillet from the heat and add the remaining broth and stir to combine. Evenly spread the meat mixture across the skillet and sprinkle the top with the cheese. Transfer the pan to the oven and bake until the cheese is hot and bubbly, about 8 minutes.
To serve, top with diced avocado, cilantro, radishes, and sliced jalapeño, and dig in with grain-free tortilla chips.

notes: Omit cheddar cheese for dairy-free or paleo. Cayenne pepper is optional.

name: Greek-Style Meatloaf
description: A dang good meatloaf with Greek-inspired ingredients — lamb, mint, oregano, and feta — topped with a tangy tomato-mustard sauce. A real crowd-pleaser.

steps: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Line a large sheet pan with parchment paper and set aside.

Melt the ghee in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the onions and 1 teaspoon of the oregano and cook, stirring, until the onion is tender, about 7 minutes. Remove from the heat and stir in the tomato paste and balsamic vinegar. Set aside to cool.

In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, ground lamb, eggs, almond flour, mint, parsley, the remaining 1 teaspoon oregano, salt, and pepper. Fold in the cooled onion mixture and the feta cheese and use a fork to lightly mix — don't mash the meat, or it will be dense. Form the mixture into an oval-shaped loaf on the prepared sheet pan, about 10 inches long and 2 inches thick.
In a small bowl, whisk together the tomato sauce, mustard, garlic, tomato paste, balsamic vinegar, salt, and cayenne pepper. Spread the sauce all over the top and sides of the meatloaf.
Bake the meatloaf for 1 hour, or until a meat thermometer inserted into the center registers 155°F. Let rest for 10 minutes before cutting into thick slices and serving.

notes: Don't mash the meat or it will be dense — use a fork to lightly mix.
Omit feta for Whole30, paleo, or dairy-free.

name: Slow Cooker Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Throw all the ingredients in a slow cooker, forget about it, then come home to a delicious, comforting dinner packed with flavor. Also includes an Instant Pot method.

steps: In a slow cooker, combine the cubed chicken, broth, onion, tomato paste, olive oil, garlic, ginger, curry powder, salt, black pepper, turmeric, cumin, paprika, cayenne pepper, and cinnamon. Stir until well combined. Place the bay leaf on top, cover, and cook on low for 6 hours or on high for 4 hours.
When the cook time is complete, in a medium bowl, whisk together the coconut milk and arrowroot starch. Add the mixture to the slow cooker with the cooked chicken and stir to combine.
Cook on high for an additional 10 minutes, uncovered and stirring occasionally, to thicken. Once the masala has thickened, stir in the lemon juice. Discard the bay leaf.
To serve, fill four bowls with rice (if desired), and divide the masala mixture among the bowls. Garnish with the cilantro and serve.

Combine the chicken, broth, onion, tomato paste, olive oil, garlic, ginger, curry powder, salt, black pepper, turmeric, cumin, paprika, cayenne pepper, and cinnamon in the Instant Pot. Cook on high pressure for 15 minutes, then quick-release.

Whisk coconut milk and arrowroot starch together. Stir into the pot, switch to sauté mode, and cook for 5 minutes until thickened. Stir in lemon juice and discard bay leaf. Serve over rice with cilantro.

notes: Adjust cayenne pepper according to your heat preference.
Instant Pot method takes about 1 hour total.

name: One-Pot Chicken Pot Pie Pasta
description: All of the flavors of classic chicken pot pie — veggies, brothy gravy, and chicken — thrown in one pot with pasta. Seriously couldn't be easier and still has that good ole comfort food feel.

steps: In a Dutch oven or pot, heat the oil over medium heat. When hot, add the onion, carrot, celery, salt, pepper, and cayenne. Cook, stirring, until the veggies are tender, about 6 minutes.

Add in the pasta, chicken broth, milk, thyme, paprika, garlic powder, and sage and stir to combine. Bring to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to a simmer and cook, uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the pasta is tender and cooked through, about 12 minutes.
Stir in the frozen peas, diced chicken, parsley, and lemon juice. Remove from the heat and cover. Let it stand until the peas are just cooked through, about 5 more minutes.
Taste and add additional seasoning, if desired. If you want your sauce a little thinner, add 1/4 cup chicken broth to thin it out.

notes: For a dairy-free option, substitute the 2 cups of milk for 1 cup of unsweetened, full-fat coconut milk and 1 cup of water.

name: One-Pot Hamburger Helper
description: A cleaned-up version of the boxed classic that's a total crowd pleaser. Just as easy as the boxed version — one pot and 25 minutes for a meal the whole family will devour.

steps: In a large, high-sided skillet or pot, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Add the beef, onion, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and paprika and cook, breaking up the beef with the back of a spoon, until the meat is browned and no longer pink, 5 to 7 minutes.

Add the tomato paste and stir until it is well combined with the beef. Stir in the pasta, broth, and milk. Bring the mixture to a boil and reduce to a simmer. Cook, stirring often, until the pasta is tender, 10 to 13 minutes.
Fold in the cheese, if using. Taste and adjust the seasoning with more salt and pepper, if desired. Sprinkle with parsley and serve hot.

notes: Use unsweetened almond milk for dairy-free and omit the cheese.
Heat leftovers over medium heat and add 1/4 cup beef broth to rehydrate the noodles until tender and creamy again.

storage.refrigerate: Reheat leftovers with a splash of beef broth to restore creaminess.

name: Creamy Cajun Chicken and Wild Rice Soup
description: A Cajun-spice twist on chicken and wild rice soup, loaded with vegetables and wild rice simmered in a delightful rich and creamy broth. A must-make if you love Cajun spices.

steps: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the onion, bell pepper, celery, and garlic and cook, stirring often, until the vegetables are tender, 5 to 7 minutes. Add the salt, black pepper, thyme, paprika, oregano, rosemary, and cayenne and cook, continuing to stir, until the spices are lightly toasted and fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the chicken broth and bay leaves. Bring the soup to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer, add the rice, cover, and cook, stirring occasionally, until the rice is tender, about 45 minutes.

When the rice is tender, in a small bowl, whisk together the coconut milk and arrowroot flour until the flour has dissolved. While gently stirring the soup, slowly pour in the creamer mixture until well combined.

Stir in the cooked chicken and continue to cook the soup, uncovered and simmering, stirring often until thickened, 5 to 10 more minutes. Stir in the lemon juice. Taste and adjust salt and pepper as desired. Discard the bay leaves.
Ladle the soup into bowls and garnish with parsley, if desired.

notes: Sub heavy cream for the coconut milk if not dairy-free.

name: Easy Italian White Bean Soup
description: An herby, hearty soup with white beans that soak up all the fantastic flavors. One of those soups that's good for the soul and a family favorite.

steps: Heat a Dutch oven or large soup pot over medium heat. Add the bacon and cook, stirring often, until the bacon is almost cooked and the fat has begun to render, 3 to 4 minutes.

Add the onion, celery, garlic, and pepper flakes and cook, stirring, until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes. Add the wine and continue to cook, stirring and scraping up any browned bits on the bottom, until the wine has reduced by half, about 2 minutes.

Add the beans, broth, Parmesan rind, rosemary, oregano, bay leaf, lemon zest, salt, and pepper and bring the soup to a boil. Reduce the heat to a light simmer, cover, and cook for about 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Discard the bay leaf and Parmesan rind. Stir in the spinach and lemon juice. Taste and add more salt and pepper if desired. Continue to cook, uncovered and simmering, about 5 more minutes.
Serve in bowls and top with the parsley, a drizzle of olive oil, and freshly grated Parmesan.

notes: Omit Parmesan rind and grated Parmesan for dairy-free.
Can sub pancetta or ham for the bacon.

name: One-Pot Cajun Chicken Pasta
description: Inspired by Chili's Cajun Chicken Pasta — big, bold Cajun spices with an easy, one-pot pasta dish with a lovely, creamy sauce. Minimal effort, maximum flavor.

steps: In a small bowl, combine the paprika, cayenne, thyme, rosemary, oregano, salt, and pepper. Stir until well combined and set aside.

Place the chicken breasts on a cutting board and cover with parchment or plastic wrap. Using a meat mallet or the bottom of a heavy skillet, pound the chicken to a uniform 1/4-inch thickness. Pat the chicken dry with a paper towel.
Season the chicken on both sides with 2 teaspoons of the prepared spice blend.

In a large deep skillet, heat the oil over medium-high heat. When hot, add the chicken and sear until golden brown on both sides and cooked through, 3 to 4 minutes per side. Transfer the cooked chicken to a cutting board and let it rest while you make the pasta.
Reduce the heat under the pan to medium. Add the onion and garlic and sauté, scraping up any browned bits and stirring until tender, about 3 minutes.

Add the pasta, chicken broth, milk, and the remaining spice blend. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook, stirring often, until the pasta is al dente and the liquid is absorbed and creamy, 12 to 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, slice the chicken into 1/4-inch strips against the grain.
Add the lemon juice to the pasta, taste, and add salt and pepper to taste. Serve in bowls and top with the sliced chicken, tomato, green onion, and Parmesan.

notes: Just 1/2 teaspoon of cayenne is spicy! For a milder flavor, go for 1/4 teaspoon. If you're serving kids, omit it altogether.
Sub almond milk for dairy-free and omit Parmesan.

name: Sheet Pan Kielbasa with Mustard Vinaigrette
description: An easy sheet pan dinner with kielbasa sausage, potatoes, and cabbage roasted until golden, then drizzled with a tangy mustard-dill vinaigrette.

steps: Preheat the oven to 400°F. Line a large rimmed sheet pan with parchment paper.
Place the potatoes on the lined sheet pan, drizzle with olive oil, and season with the salt and pepper. Roast for 15 minutes.
Remove the pan from the oven. Add the kielbasa, cabbage, and onion. Using a spatula, gently toss until combined with the oil. Spread into a single layer and bake for another 20 minutes.
Remove from the oven and pour the beer over the sheet pan, coating all the ingredients. Carefully return the sheet pan to the oven and cook until the cabbage has browned, about 10 more minutes.
Meanwhile, in a small bowl, combine the olive oil, whole-grain mustard, Dijon mustard, chopped dill, and black pepper.
Serve with lemon wedges, garnish with fresh dill fronds, and drizzle with the mustard vinaigrette.

name: Green Chile Pork Medallions
description: Pork tenderloin medallions served over creamy mashed potatoes with a rich Hatch green chile gravy. A delicious way to enjoy Hatch chile season any time of year.

steps: Boil the potatoes in salted water until fork-tender, about 15 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Melt 2 tablespoons butter with the garlic and creamer. Mash the potatoes and stir in the butter mixture until smooth.
Slice the pork tenderloin into 1-inch medallions. Season with salt and pepper, then dredge in cassava flour.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the pork medallions until golden brown on both sides, 2 to 3 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.

In the same skillet, melt the butter. Add the garlic and cook until fragrant. Add the wine and scrape up the browned bits. Add the green chiles, chicken broth, cumin, and oregano. Simmer until reduced and thickened, about 10 minutes.
Return the pork medallions to the sauce and cook for 2 minutes to warm through.
Serve the pork medallions over the mashed potatoes, spooned with the green chile gravy, and garnish with cilantro.

notes: Use ghee instead of butter for dairy-free. Use broth instead of wine for paleo.

name: Panang Curry-Inspired Chicken Meatballs
description: Ground chicken meatballs with Thai basil and almond flour, simmered in a dreamy Panang curry sauce with coconut milk, almond butter, and red curry paste.

steps: In a large bowl, combine the ground chicken, Thai basil, egg, salt, pepper, and almond flour. Mix until just combined. Using damp hands, form the mixture into 2-inch meatballs.

In a large deep skillet, heat the avocado oil over medium-high heat until shimmering. Add the meatballs, working in batches as needed, and cook on each side until golden brown, 2 to 3 minutes per side. Transfer the browned meatballs to a plate and set aside.

Reduce the heat under the skillet to medium. Add the shallot, garlic, and ginger and cook, stirring often, until the shallot is tender, 2 to 3 minutes. Add the red curry paste and continue to cook until the paste is softened and fragrant, about 2 minutes.

While whisking, slowly pour in the coconut milk and continue to whisk until the mixture is clump-free. Add the broth, almond butter, coconut aminos, and fish sauce. Bring the sauce to a simmer and continue whisking until the sauce is very creamy, 1 to 2 minutes.
Nestle the meatballs into the sauce and simmer, covered, until the meatballs are cooked through and the flavors have melded, about 10 minutes.
Stir in the lime juice and Thai basil. Serve over rice, if desired, and with a wedge of lime.

notes: Use damp hands when forming meatballs to keep the meat from sticking.
If you prefer peanut butter instead of almond butter, use only 2 tablespoons. Peanut butter is not considered Whole30 or paleo compatible.

name: Thai-Inspired Chicken Fried Rice
description: A fresh, flavorful take on fried rice with Thai-inspired seasonings — fish sauce, coconut aminos, Thai basil, and cilantro. Ready in just 20 minutes.

steps: In a large nonstick skillet, heat the avocado oil over medium-high heat. Add the garlic, shallot, and Thai chiles and cook, stirring, until tender, about 3 minutes. Add the carrots and cook another 2 minutes.
Add the cooked rice and cook, stirring to combine, about 2 minutes. Add the coconut aminos and fish sauce and stir to coat.
Push the rice to one half of the skillet. Pour the eggs into the empty half and cook, stirring, until softly scrambled. Stir the scrambled eggs into the rice until well combined.
Add the chicken, salt, white pepper, and green onions. Cook until heated through.
Remove the skillet from the heat and stir in the lime juice, cilantro, and Thai basil.
Serve topped with cucumber slices, sesame seeds, and a wedge of lime.

notes: When cooking any sort of fried rice, day-old rice works best. If using freshly cooked rice, spread it on a sheet pan to cool and dry out before frying.
Use 1 chile for mild, 3 for hot.

name: Clayton's Favorite Mustard Salmon
description: A salmon dish that's very easy to prepare but makes you feel like you're dining at a great restaurant. Served over cauliflower puree with a tangy mustard sauce and crispy shallots.

steps: In a small bowl, stir together the grainy mustard, Dijon, pepperoncini brine, and thyme leaves. Refrigerate until ready to serve.

In a medium saucepan, combine the cauliflower, garlic, broth, thyme, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Reduce the heat to a simmer, cover, and cook until the cauliflower is fork-tender, 12 to 14 minutes.
Carefully transfer the cauliflower, along with the contents of the saucepan, to a blender or food processor. Add the coconut milk and blend until smooth, about 30 seconds. Cover to keep warm.

In a large nonstick skillet, heat 2 tablespoons oil over medium-high heat. Pat the salmon fillets dry and season all sides with salt and pepper. When the skillet is hot but not smoking, sear the salmon on one side until golden brown, about 3 minutes. Flip and cook to your desired doneness, about 3 to 4 minutes more. Transfer to a plate and set aside.

Reduce the heat to medium-low. If needed, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil. Combine the sliced shallots, cassava flour, and salt and toss until well coated. Spread the shallots in a single layer in the skillet and fry until crisp, 2 to 3 minutes, flipping halfway through. Transfer to a plate lined with paper towels.
Smear the cauliflower puree onto individual plates, spreading it into a thin layer. Place the salmon on top. Spoon the mustard sauce over the salmon and top with the crispy shallots.

storage.refrigerate: Mustard sauce keeps well for up to 2 weeks in the refrigerator.

notes: Sub 1 teaspoon apple cider vinegar for the pepperoncini brine for Whole30.

name: Tomato-Basil Tortellini and Sausage Soup
description: A simple, comforting tortellini soup with Italian sausage, kale, basil, and a touch of creaminess from coconut milk. The whole family will ask for it over and over again.

steps: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Add the sausage and cook, breaking up the meat with the edge of a spoon, until cooked through and no longer pink, 5 to 7 minutes. Drain off all but about 2 tablespoons fat from the pot.
Add the onion, garlic, salt, pepper, and Italian seasoning. Cook, stirring, until the onion is tender, about 4 minutes.

Add the tomato paste and stir to combine. Add the crushed tomatoes, vegetable broth, coconut milk, and basil. Stir to combine and bring to a rapid simmer. Reduce the heat to a light simmer and let cook, uncovered and stirring occasionally, for about 5 minutes.
Stir in the kale and tortellini. Continue to cook, uncovered, until the tortellini are tender and the kale has wilted, about 4 more minutes.
Remove from heat and serve with additional freshly ground black pepper, if desired.

storage.makeAhead: Make the soup base in advance, but do not add the kale or tortellini. When heating up to serve, bring to a simmer, add the kale and tortellini, and cook for 4 minutes until the pasta is tender and the kale has wilted. When stored, the tortellini will absorb some of the liquid, so add more broth as needed when reheating.

name: One-Pan Coconut-Lime Chicken and Rice
description: Roasted bone-in chicken thighs over fluffy rice enhanced by Thai-inspired flavors — coconut milk, fish sauce, garlic, ginger, and Thai chiles. A dreamy one-pan dinner.

steps: Preheat the oven to 350°F.

Pat the chicken dry with a paper towel. In a small bowl, combine the salt, black pepper, and lime zest. Rub the mixture into the skin side of the chicken thighs, pressing the seasoning in so the lime zest sticks.

In a large deep ovenproof skillet with a fitted lid, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the chicken skin side down in a single layer and cook undisturbed until golden brown and crispy, 6 to 7 minutes. Transfer to a plate and set aside.

In the same skillet, reduce the heat to medium-low. Discard all but 2 tablespoons of fat. Add the shallot, garlic, ginger, and chiles and sauté until tender, about 1 minute. Add the rice, white parts of the green onion, and salt. Cook, stirring, toasting the rice until fragrant and lightly browned, 2 to 3 minutes.
Stir in the lime zest, lime juice, fish sauce, cilantro, coconut milk, and chicken broth. Bring to a simmer. Nestle the chicken thighs back into the rice, skin side up.
Cover the skillet, transfer to the oven, and bake for 25 minutes. Uncover and continue baking until the rice is tender and the chicken is cooked through with crisp skin, 10 more minutes.
Remove from the oven, fluff the rice with a fork, and let rest for 5 minutes. Garnish with the green parts of the green onion and cilantro leaves. Serve with fresh lime wedges.

equipment: large deep ovenproof skillet with lid
notes: Use 1 chile for mild, 3 for hot.

name: Sour Cream Chicken Enchiladas
description: Adapted from a childhood recipe — grain-free tortillas filled with shredded chicken and green chiles, smothered in a creamy sour cream sauce and baked until bubbly.

steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F. Grease a 9 x 13-inch baking dish with butter.

In a small saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the tapioca flour and whisk until just combined. Add the cumin and salt and whisk to combine. Toast the cumin for about 2 minutes, stirring constantly.

Slowly add the chicken broth, whisking constantly until well incorporated with the tapioca mixture, then continue to whisk while you pour in 1 cup of the salsa verde. Cook, still whisking constantly, until the sauce thickens, 1 to 2 minutes.
Remove from the heat and whisk in the sour cream until smooth. Set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the shredded chicken, 1/2 cup of the sour cream sauce, the diced green chiles, 1/2 cup Monterey Jack, and salt. Stir until well combined.

Spread 1/2 cup of the sour cream sauce in the prepared baking dish to coat the bottom. One at a time, warm the tortillas in a dry skillet for about 30 seconds per side. Fill each with about 1/4 cup filling, roll up, and place seam side down in the dish.
Pour the remaining sour cream sauce over the enchiladas. Top with the remaining 1/2 cup cheese, the remaining salsa verde, and jalapeño slices.
Bake, uncovered, until the cheese is melted and the sauce is bubbling, about 20 minutes. Turn the oven to broil and cook until the cheese is golden and bubbling, 2 to 4 minutes.
Let cool for 5 minutes. Top with cilantro and red onion and serve.

notes: The sauce might feel overly thick, but don't worry — as it cooks, the consistency works perfectly.

name: Slow Cooker Beer-Braised Chicken Tacos
description: Chicken slowly simmered in a chipotle-beer sauce, then shredded for tacos. Served with an avocado crema. Great for busy weeknights with leftovers, or for hosting a football-watching party.

steps: In a small bowl, combine the salt, smoked paprika, cumin, garlic powder, black pepper, and oregano. Season both sides of the chicken with the seasoning mix.

In a large skillet, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Working in batches, sear the chicken until golden brown on both sides, 2 to 3 minutes per side. The chicken does not need to be cooked through at this stage. Set aside.

In a slow cooker, combine the chipotle peppers, tomato paste, green chiles, and beer and whisk until well combined. Nestle the chicken into the sauce, cover, and cook on high for 4 hours or on low for 6 hours.
Transfer the chicken to a sheet pan. Using two forks, shred the chicken, then return it to the slow cooker and stir to coat with the sauce.
Blend the avocado, pickled jalapeños, jalapeño brine, cilantro, garlic, lime juice, and salt until smooth.
Serve the shredded chicken in corn tortillas or lettuce cups, topped with the avocado crema, shredded lettuce, radishes, cilantro, and lime wedges.

notes: Pressure cooker method also available: sear chicken, then cook on high pressure for 15 minutes with quick release.
Don't discard the liquid in the slow cooker — return shredded chicken to it for maximum flavor.

name: Jerk-Inspired Chicken Tacos
description: A little jerk twist on Taco Tuesday — shredded chicken flavored with a jerk-style homemade spice blend, paired with a pineapple pico and creamy avocado mash.

steps: In a medium bowl, combine the diced pineapple, jalapeño, red onion, avocado oil, lime juice, salt, and pepper. Stir to combine. Set aside.
Scoop the avocado into a medium bowl and add the lime juice, cilantro, garlic, and salt. Using the back of a fork, mash until mostly smooth. Set aside.

In a large skillet, heat the avocado oil over medium heat. Add the chicken, thyme, allspice, cayenne, garlic powder, ground ginger, salt, and black pepper. Stir until the chicken is well coated in the spices. Spread in a single layer and cook undisturbed until a golden crust has formed, 3 to 4 minutes.
Add the broth and lime juice. Continue to cook, stirring, until the chicken has mostly absorbed the broth, 2 to 3 minutes. Reduce the heat to low to keep warm while you warm the tortillas.
If using tortillas, warm each one in a dry skillet over medium heat for about 30 seconds per side. You can also use tongs to char the tortillas directly over a gas flame for a few seconds.
For each taco, smear a small amount of the avocado mash on a warm tortilla and fill with the chicken. Garnish with pineapple pico and cilantro. Serve with lime wedges.

notes: Save time by using a rotisserie chicken for the shredded chicken.

name: Greek-Inspired Chicken and Orzo Bake
description: Boldly seasoned chicken, crisp asparagus, juicy tomatoes, and orzo come together for a healthy and delicious Greek-inspired one-skillet dinner. Simple, mouthwatering, and packed with Mediterranean flavors.

steps: Preheat the oven to 400°F.
In a small bowl, combine 1 teaspoon of the salt, 1/2 teaspoon of the oregano, the black pepper, paprika, cumin, and cayenne. Pat the chicken dry and season with the spice blend on both sides evenly.

In a large deep ovenproof skillet (preferably cast-iron), heat the oil over medium-high heat. Working in batches if necessary, add the chicken in a single layer and cook until golden brown on both sides, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer to a plate and set aside.

Reduce the heat to medium, add the garlic, shallot, asparagus, cherry tomatoes, and remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt. Cook, stirring, until the tomatoes begin to soften and the asparagus turns bright green, about 4 minutes.

Pour in the wine and deglaze the skillet, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Cook, stirring, until the wine is reduced by half, about 2 minutes. Add the orzo, artichoke hearts, broth, and remaining 1 teaspoon oregano and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then nestle the browned chicken into the skillet and sprinkle the olives over the entire skillet.

Transfer to the oven and bake, uncovered, for 10 minutes. Remove from the oven and crumble the feta over the chicken. Return to the oven and bake until the chicken is cooked through and the orzo is tender, 10 to 12 more minutes.
Let cool for 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with the fresh parsley.

notes: Use gluten-free orzo for a gluten-free version. Omit feta for dairy-free.
